title: Sphaerostephanos erectus
taxonomy: Sphaerostephanos erectus is classified within the plant family Dryopteridaceae, which comprises ferns. It belongs to the genus Sphaerostephanos, a group of ferns commonly found in tropical and subtropical regions. The species S. erectus is further classified under the kingdom Plantae and the phylum Pteridophyta. The classification of S. erectus is as follows: kingdom - Plantae, phylum - Pteridophyta, class - Polypodiopsida, order - Polypodiales, family - Dryopteridaceae, genus - Sphaerostephanos, species - S. erectus. This classification helps in understanding the evolutionary relationships and characteristics of the species.

intro: Sphaerostephanos erectus, commonly known as the Erect Sphaerostephanos, is a type of fern native to tropical regions, specifically in Southeast Asia. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, offering potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and respiratory issues.
